description: Formal register of compliance areas not yet adequately represented in the document repository. This file is read by the system prompt to ensure Claude explicitly flags gaps rather than retrieving weak matches. Any query touching a registered gap area must trigger an explicit acknowledgement and signpost to appropriate external guidance.

gaps.description: Employment law obligations for Scottish charities — contracts of employment, disciplinary and grievance procedures, absence management, redundancy, TUPE where relevant. Applies to any charity with paid staff.
gaps.why_it_matters: Any charity with paid staff has employment law obligations under UK-wide legislation regardless of size or income. Absence of this area from the repository means the system cannot support HR policy development.
gaps.notes: Priority for next repository enhancement. Most Scottish charities with paid staff will need at minimum a disciplinary and grievance procedure and written contracts.

gaps.description: Health and safety obligations for charities operating from premises or delivering activities in physical settings — H&S policy, risk assessments, fire safety, lone working, event safety.
gaps.why_it_matters: Any charity with employees or that invites members of the public onto premises has legal H&S obligations. Particularly critical for charities delivering direct services.
relevant_legislation: Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974
Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999
Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences Regulations 2013 (RIDDOR)
external_guidance: Scottish Fire and Rescue Service guidance
gaps.notes: Relevant to any charity with premises, staff, volunteers working on-site, or public-facing activities. Risk assessment templates are the most commonly needed output.

gaps.description: Social media policies, public communications standards, media handling procedures, brand and reputational risk management.
gaps.why_it_matters: Reputational risk is significant for charities. OSCR expects charities to manage public trust. Social media misuse by trustees, staff or volunteers can create regulatory and legal exposure.
relevant_legislation: UK GDPR — where communications involve personal data
Charities and Trustee Investment (Scotland) Act 2005 — public benefit and reputational obligations
external_guidance: Charity Commission for England and Wales guidance on social media (useful reference even outwith jurisdiction)
gaps.notes: Lower immediate priority than employment and H&S but increasingly relevant as charities use social media for fundraising and campaigning.

gaps.description: Fundraising practice standards, donor management, gift aid administration, public collections, online fundraising compliance.
gaps.why_it_matters: Fundraising is a primary income source for many Scottish charities. The Fundraising Regulator sets standards that apply UK-wide. Gift aid carries HMRC obligations.
relevant_legislation: Civic Government (Scotland) Act 1982 — street and public collections
gaps.notes: Partial coverage exists. Priority is to add gift aid procedure templates and online fundraising compliance guidance.

gaps.description: Volunteer management frameworks, volunteer agreements, induction and training, expenses policies, volunteer boundaries and conduct.
gaps.why_it_matters: Volunteers are central to the Scottish charity sector. Clear volunteer management reduces safeguarding risk, supports retention, and protects the organisation legally.
relevant_legislation: There is no single piece of legislation governing volunteers in the UK
Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Scotland) Act 2007 — where volunteers work with protected groups
Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974 — extends to volunteers in many circumstances
gaps.notes: Partial coverage exists. Most needed additions are volunteer agreement templates and expenses policy examples scaled to different organisational sizes.

gaps.description: Grant reporting obligations, funder compliance requirements, restricted fund management, grant conditions monitoring.
gaps.why_it_matters: Grant income is the primary funding source for many Scottish charities. Failure to comply with grant conditions can result in clawback and reputational damage. Restricted fund management has specific accounting and governance implications.
relevant_legislation: Charities and Trustee Investment (Scotland) Act 2005 — financial management obligations
external_guidance: OSCR guidance on charity accounts and financial management
National Lottery Community Fund compliance requirements
gaps.notes: Partial coverage exists focused on larger funders. Needs expansion to cover smaller grant makers and internal restricted fund management procedures.

gap_response_instruction: When a user query relates to a compliance area registered as 'gap' or 'partial' in this file, Claude must: (1) explicitly acknowledge the limitation, (2) provide whatever general guidance is possible without fabricating specific compliance requirements, (3) direct the user to the listed external guidance sources, and (4) recommend independent professional advice where the area carries significant legal risk.
